score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model confidently asserts crypto-tax meanings with no uncertainty signal and no connection to the canonical notation-design meaning.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold: cleanly separates crypto token tax from AI API token cost (different domains). Post: explicitly distinguishes architectural verbosity cost vs. monetary API billing, matching canonical framing.

Cold: distinguishes crypto tax as intentional fee vs. bloat as waste, but in wrong domain (no notation/structure framing). Post: clearly frames Token Tax as justified structural overhead vs. valueless bloat, aligned with canonical definition.

Cold: distinguishes financial vs. mental cost but entirely in crypto domain, missing canonical notation-design meaning. Post: explicitly identifies Token Tax as the investment that reduces cognitive load — the canonical cause/effect relationship.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: Cold response asserted crypto-tax/DeFi/software meanings as established meanings without uncertainty and did not identify the coined notation-design meaning.
per_dimension_justifications: Vs token cost/API cost-per-token: cold distinction was only by confabulated crypto-vs-LLM domain separation; post-introduction cleanly distinguished architectural token-overhead tradeoff from monetary API billing.

Vs bloat/overhead: cold response partially captured intentional valuable cost vs waste, though in the wrong crypto frame; post-introduction cleanly named justified overhead for structure, auditability, type safety, and reduced ambiguity versus valueless bloat.

Vs cognitive load: cold response separated by wrong financial-vs-mental framing; post-introduction cleanly stated Token Tax is explicit notation overhead paid to reduce cognitive load/free reasoning headroom.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model confidently asserts three incorrect definitions related to cryptocurrency and a specific company, presenting them as an exhaustive breakdown of the term's uses without expressing any uncertainty or acknowledging it might be a neologism.
per_dimension_justifications: In the cold state, the model hard-confabulated definitions related to cryptocurrency. For all three near-neighbors, it successfully distinguished its *confabulated* definition from the neighbor. However, since the basis for the distinction was entirely incorrect and unrelated to the canonical definition, these all score as 'accidental separation' (score 1).

After receiving the canonical definition, the model demonstrated deep comprehension. For all three near-neighbors, it provided clean, substantive distinctions perfectly aligned with the canonical definition. It correctly identified Token Tax as a conceptual design trade-off vs. a financial billing model (API cost), a deliberate feature vs. unintentional waste (bloat), and an investment made to reduce its counterpart (cognitive load).

Post-introduction, the model explicitly and correctly named the non-trivial distinguishing features for each near-neighbor. It contrasted the architectural/conceptual nature of Token Tax with the financial nature of API cost; the deliberate/value-add nature of Token Tax with the unintentional/wasteful nature of bloat; and the cause/solution nature of Token Tax with the problem/effect nature of cognitive load.
